dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 17 responding for chest pain.
Hi, I'm Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Chest pain, sharp and tender, on my left side. Feel like a 5 out of 10.
Mr. David, can you tell me your name and today's date?
David... четверг, I think.
Can you open your eyes and look at me?
Eyes are open and alert.
Tap your right index finger for me.
Hmmm... okay.
Pulse is 111, strong and regular.
Airway is patent and clear. Any difficulty breathing?
No wheezing or coughing, chest feels a bit tight but no shortness of breath.
Any recent shortness of breath or chest tightness at all?
No, not really.
Breathing appears adequate. Can I check your capillary refill and skin condition?
Capillary nail bed refill is less than 2 seconds. Skin is warm, dry, and normal color. No cyanosis noted.
Blood pressure is 153 over 93. No signs of hypotension at this time.
SpO2 is 95. Pulse ox is trending in normal limits.
Blood glucose is 116.
Was this chest pain the first time you've felt it or have you had something similar before?
Not like this, but I have had COPD flares before. This is sharper and more localized on my left side.
What medicines are you currently taking to manage your conditions?
I take Amlodipine, Lisinopril, Aspirin, and Hydrochlorothiazide.
Have you ever been diagnosed with any chronic conditions or substance use disorder issues in the past?

Yes, I have a history of COPD, liver disease, hypertension, GERD, and I have had past issues with drug and alcohol use including opioids, alcohol, and cocaine. I also had bacterial pneumonia and an inguinal hernia repaired previously.
Did you eat or drink anything before the chest pain started?
Three beers approximately an hour ago. I haven't eaten anything else recently. No recent drug use.
The chest pain started while you were walking, correct? Can you be more specific about the exact location and time frame you began to feel it?

Yes, it hit while I was walking on the sidewalk near the store. I don't recall the exact time, maybe 30-45 minutes ago? The left middle of the chest, and feels comfortably supported now despite being tender to touch.
Would you describe the pain as stabbing, tightness, pressure-like, or something else perhaps?
It started out more like a hammer hit me. Now it feels like a dull ache with almost a heavy sensation in the center of my left chest.
Has the pain traveled anywhere else in your body or stayed in one place?
It's still in the middle left side of the chest. Doesn't go down my arm or anything.
On a scale from 0 to 10, with 10 being unbearable, what level would you say your pain is?
Maybe a 5 now. It came on quickly and hasn't peaked yet.
Skin is warm, dry, and intact. No bruises or rashes detected. Any indication of jaundice or pallor?
No, I don't have any discoloration. Skin looks normal to me.
May I shine a penlight in your eyes for a routine check of your pupils?
Yeah, go ahead.
Pupils are pinpoint.
Open your mouth a bit for me. Are you experiencing any pressure or pain in the throat?
No pain in the back of the throat. No numbness or burning either.
Any discomfort or dryness in your nose or ears?
No, everything feels normal.
We need to listen to your lungs. Can you take slow and deep breaths for me now?
Equal bilateral chest rise and fall. Breath sounds are equal and clear bilaterally.
Let's apply the 4-lead monitor first, partner.
ECG monitor applied with normal positioning. Preliminary interpretation is sinus tachycardia.
I'll now take a more accurate count of your breathing.
Marking 18 breaths per minute now.
No signs of retractions, use of accessory muscles, or labored breathing.
Can you lie just a bit back so we can properly position you for the full 12-lead EKG?
12-lead ECG is applied. Initial interpretation shows sinus tachycardia.
The current EKG interpretation shows sinus tach, left atrial enlargement, nonspecific T wave changes, and moderate right axis deviation.
I will gently palpate your chest for further assessment of pain.
It is tender in the middle of the left side, just a bit below the breast line.
Do you feel any changes in chest contour, deformity, or abnormal breathing patterns on one side?
No, it just feels tender to touch.
Positive PMS for pain found upon palpation in the left mid-chest.
We are following protocol 3-15 for altered mental status from the GCS of 14.
GCS is 14, as contributed by eye opening 3, verbal response 5, and motor response 6.
EKG results show sinus tach with no new or acute ST changes.
Will you be able to travel to Richmond Community Hospital without issues?
Yeah, sure. I'm in no pain right now to prevent it.
Let me assist you into the ambulance and onto the stretcher, then we'll secure you in.
Is the patient seated and secured on the stretcher.
Let me take a follow-up round: Pulse now is 102, respiratory at 16, blood pressure is 145/95.
SpO2 was lost due to a sensor issue; we are reattaching a sensor.
Reassessed EKG; still showing sinus tach. No acute changes noted.
Transport under Protocol 3-15 as allocated for an altered mental status with GCS 14.
We have contact with Richmond Community ER en route and will inform them of all findings.
The patient is now stabilized for transport and info is en route with the EKG.
ED has been informed. All clinical findings and vital signs are being relayed during transport.
No changes in patient condition en route.
Did I miss mentioning any additional conditions or symptoms during your interview?
I mentioned GERD before and also an inguinal hernia.
You are assessed as NARD. Would you like support moving into the stretcher at the ER or could you walk?
I can walk.
